editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Lucy Ramsden (1862-1928) to the chairman of the joint committee of the Royal College of Physicians (RCPI) and Royal College of Surgeons (RCSI). Ramsden requests that a deputation from the Irish Nurses' Association be allowed to attend a meeting to discuss the formation of a nursing college, similar to one established in England by Sir Arthur Stanley.

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

correspAction.placeName: Royal College of Physicians, Kildare Street, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
THE IRISH NURSES' ASSOCIATION 34 ST. STEPHEN'S GREEN, Dublin. March 22nd, 1916, 191 To the Chairman Joint Committee of the Royal College of Physicians and The Royal College of Surgeons Ireland. Sir, I have heard that you purpose holding a Meeting to consider
the Hon. Arthur Stanley's Scheme re 'A College of Nursing.' On behalf of the Irish Nurses Association I venture to ask
if you would receive a deputation from the Association and so
give the Members an opportunity of placing their views on this
Subject before you? Trusting you will see your way to granting this request. I am, Sir
Yours very faithfully, (Signed) Lucy Ramsden
(President. Irish Nurses' Association)
